paper_assessments.experimental_system: human; T cell line (TCR-engineered T cells) plus hepatocellular carcinoma cells and murine model
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: TCR-engineered T cells targeting FOXM1 262-270 efficiently eradicate tumor cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: FOXM1 is used as a tumor antigen for TCR-engineered T cells that kill cancer cells; no evidence FOXM1 regulates TCR signalling or an inhibitory T cell phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human/non-human cancer cell lines; non-immune system
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: ABL1 phosphorylates FOXM1, increasing stability and promoting FOXM1-dependent tumorigenicity.
paper_assessments.rationale: Mechanistic study of ABL1-mediated FOXM1 stabilization and tumor growth in cancer cells; no T cells or TCR-related phenotype examined.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human NSCLC cells and murine syngeneic tumor model; non-immune (tumor) system with tumor-infiltrating T cell readout
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: FOXM1 selectively upregulates PD-L1 expression by binding directly to the PD-L1 promoter.
paper_assessments.rationale: FOXM1 knockdown/inhibition lowers tumor PD-L1 (an inhibitory checkpoint ligand) and improves immunotherapy, but FOXM1 acts tumor-intrinsically, not in T cells; no direct TCR-signalling readout.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human gastric cancer cells co-cultured with human CD8+ T cells and nude mouse model
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: FOXM1 up-regulates PD-L1, hinders CD8+ T cell activation and promotes immune escape.
paper_assessments.rationale: FOXM1 knockdown reduces Notch-driven PD-L1 and restores CD8+ T cell proliferation/cytotoxicity, giving causal evidence, but FOXM1 is tumor-intrinsic and readout is checkpoint-mediated activation, not proximal TCR signalling.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human lung adenocarcinoma cells; non-immune system
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: NEIL3, transcriptionally activated by FOXM1, inhibits ferroptosis in these cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: FOXM1/NEIL3 axis regulates ferroptosis in tumor cells; no T cell or TCR involvement.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human breast cancer cells (MCF-7); non-immune system
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: FDI-6 displaces FOXM1 from genomic targets and selectively downregulates its target genes.
paper_assessments.rationale: Small-molecule FOXM1 inhibitor discovery/characterization in cancer cells; no T cell or TCR inhibition data.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human follicular lymphoma cell lines and CDX/PDX models; non-immune (tumor) system
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: PPARα transcriptionally inhibits FOXM1 expression, causing downregulation of downstream target genes.
paper_assessments.rationale: PPARα-FOXM1 axis in lymphoma tumor cell proliferation/apoptosis; no TCR or inhibitory T cell phenotype assessed.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human cancer cell lines of various origin; non-immune system
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Suppressing FOXM1 sensitized cancer cells to DNA-damage-induced apoptosis.
paper_assessments.rationale: FOXM1 suppression enhances DNA-damage apoptosis in cancer cells; unrelated to T cells or TCR signalling.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human mesothelioma cell lines; non-immune system
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Baicalein suppresses mesothelioma growth through modulation of the p53-FOXM1 signaling axis.
paper_assessments.rationale: Baicalein modulates p53-FOXM1 in mesothelioma proliferation/invasion; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human T cells and human/murine SCLC cell lines and xenograft/spontaneous mouse models; CD8+ cytotoxic T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: FOXM1 inhibition enhanced T cell activation and supported differentiation of CD8+ cytotoxic T cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: FOXM1 inhibition enhances T cell activation and CD8+ differentiation, implying FOXM1 restrains activation, but this is generic activation/differentiation, not TCR inhibition, anergy or checkpoint attenuation.

parsed.tier_rationale: Two primary studies (PMC12515241, PMC9561767) provide perturbational evidence (FOXM1 knockdown/inhibition) that FOXM1 upregulates the PD-L1 inhibitory checkpoint and thereby suppresses CD8+ T cell activation and function, with functional readouts. This is indirect mechanistic evidence materially connecting FOXM1 to an inhibitory checkpoint T cell phenotype, but FOXM1 acts tumor-intrinsically rather than within T cells, and no study demonstrates direct proximal TCR-signalling inhibition, anergy or hyporesponsiveness. Directness is therefore incomplete, so evidence sits at moderate rather than strong; remaining relevant paper (PMC12236922) shows only generic T cell activation/differentiation.
